task: Two tasks: (1) CONTENT — Review the /pricing page (src/indiestack/routes/pricing.py). Check copy accuracy, any stale claims or broken promises. Also look at the /submit page — is the CTA clear and trust-building? Fix any obvious copy issues. (2) MCP — audit the top 5 most-called MCP tools (find_tools, get_tool_details, build_stack, scan_project, list_categories) by reading their tool descriptions in mcp_server.py. Are the descriptions accurate and compelling? Do they mention the 8,000+ tool catalog? Are there any description improvements that would make the tools more discoverable or useful to AI agents? Propose and implement any improvements.

assignments.content: Review src/indiestack/routes/pricing.py for copy accuracy — check for stale claims, broken promises, outdated stats (tool counts, pricing figures). Also review the /submit page (src/indiestack/routes/submit.py) — assess whether the CTA is clear and trust-building. Fix any obvious copy issues directly in the files.
assignments.mcp: Audit the top 5 MCP tool descriptions in src/indiestack/mcp_server.py: find_tools, get_tool_details, build_stack, scan_project, list_categories. Check: (1) are descriptions accurate and compelling? (2) do they mention the 8,000+ tool catalog? (3) are there improvements that would make tools more discoverable/useful to AI agents? Propose and implement improvements directly in mcp_server.py.

decomposition.reasoning: Two independent tasks with no cross-department dependencies — content reviews/fixes copy on pricing and submit pages, MCP audits and improves tool descriptions in mcp_server.py. Both can run fully in parallel.

strategy_review.reasoning: Two well-scoped, non-overlapping tasks with no production DB changes, no deploys, and no destructive operations. Content reviewing pricing.py and submit.py is fresh scope — the 04-04 15:17 content audit covered landing/explore/tool pages but NOT pricing or submit. MCP auditing tool descriptions in mcp_server.py is also fresh — previous MCP audits checked API response quality and search quality, not the tool description strings themselves. Both are read-heavy tasks with targeted copy fixes. Only risk is the MCP department implementing description changes without flagging the PyPI publish requirement.

approved_tasks.content: Review src/indiestack/routes/pricing.py for copy accuracy — check for stale claims, broken promises, outdated stats (tool counts, pricing figures). Cross-check any numeric claims against production DB if possible. Also review src/indiestack/routes/submit.py — assess whether the CTA is clear and trust-building. Fix any obvious copy issues directly in the files.
approved_tasks.mcp: Audit the top 5 MCP tool descriptions in src/indiestack/mcp_server.py: find_tools, get_tool_details, build_stack, scan_project, list_categories. Check: (1) are descriptions accurate and compelling? (2) do they mention the 8,000+ tool catalog? (3) are there improvements that would make tools more discoverable/useful to AI agents? Propose and implement improvements directly in mcp_server.py. IMPORTANT: Note that any changes to mcp_server.py require a separate PyPI publish to take effect — do NOT deploy, just make the code changes and flag that a /publish-mcp is needed.

conditions: MCP department must NOT attempt a PyPI publish or deploy — only edit the file and note that /publish-mcp is needed afterward
Content department should verify any stat claims (tool counts, install numbers) against what's in the codebase rather than hardcoding new numbers they can't verify
risk_flags: Gotcha: stats in copy go stale fast — pricing page may claim outdated tool counts. Content dept should flag any numbers they can't verify rather than guessing new ones
Gotcha: MCP changes need PyPI publish to take effect — without it, edits are dead code until someone remembers to publish
